title: Microbiome analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7iei4","text":"This protocol describes the microbiome analysis of ruminal or in vitro rumen samples. It includes sample collection, DNA extraction using bead-beating and column purification, PCR amplification of the 16S rRNA gene, and amplicon purification. The purified DNA is quantified and sent for sequencing to assess microbial community composition.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":62,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare the magnetic stand for the AMPure XP beads. Use hot glue to fix one magnet each in the north-south direction between two holes under the PCR rack.
From the daily feed waste, collect 2 x 2 mL of samples using a cut-off pipette tip to transfer both liquid and solid contents.
Discard the supernatant and freeze the pellet at -80 °C until further processing.
Separate the solids from the liquid by centrifugation step at 10000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Thaw the pellets On ice and add 0.3 g of 0.1 mm beads and 0.1 g of 0.5 mm beads.
Add 1.5 µL of DNase-free RNase and incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 .
Add 200 µL of 10 Molarity (M) NH 4 CH 3 COO ( 260 µL if the optional step was carried out), mix well and incubate On ice for 00:05:00 .
Transfer the supernatant to a new Eppendorf tube. Optional: Add another 300 µL lysis buffer to the pellet and repeat steps 7-10 if needed.
Centrifuge at 16000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Incubate at 70 °C for 00:15:00 , shaking gently every 5 minutes using a thermomixer.
Homogenize the pellets using a bead beater at maximum speed for 00:03:00 .
Dissolve the nucleic acid pellet in 150 µL of elution buffer from the kit.
Discard the supernatant, wash the nucleic acid pellet with ice-cold 70% ethanol and let it dry.
Centrifuge at 16000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Transfer 1 mL of the supernatant to a new 2 mL Eppendorf tube, add an equal volume of ice-cold isopropanol, mix well and incubate On ice for 00:30:00 .
Centrifuge at 16000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Discard the flow-through, add 500 µL of Buffer GW2 and centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .
Prepare a PCR-Mastermix: A B 1 5x Q5 reaction buffer 10 µL 2 10 mM dNTP's 1 µL 3 Forward primer 2.5 µL 4 Reverse primer 2.5 µL 5 Q5 HiFi DNA polymerase 0.5 µL 6 ddH2O 28.5 µL 7 One reaction 40 µL
For the amplification of the 16S small subunit ribosomal RNA fragments following primers were used: %s %s

Seal the individual reaction tubes and perform the PCR using the following settings: 95 °C for 00:03:00 35 cycles of: 95 °C for 00:00:30 55 °C for 00:00:30 72 °C for 00:00:30 72 °C for 00:05:00 Hold at 4 °C
Centrifuge the amplicon PCR plate at 1000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute to collect the condensate, then carefully remove the seal. Allow the beads to air‐dry for 00:10:00 or until all ethanol has evaporated.
Add 15 µL of proteinase K and 250 µL of Buffer GL, mix well, and incubate at 55 °C for 00:10:00 . Vortex every 60s.
Add 400 µL of GB-Buffer, mix well and spin down any residues by centrifuging at 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Transfer the supernatant to an ExtractMe column and centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Discard the flow-through. Take a new tube, add 600 µL of Buffer GW1 and centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .
Add 1 µL of GelRed, pour the gel into the mold, insert the comb and let it cool.
Prepare an agarose gel by boiling 0.4 g of agarose in 50 mL TAE Buffer. Refill the evaporated water.
Discard the flow-through and dry the column by centrifugation at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .
Transfer the column to a new Eppendorf tube. Add 200 µL of warm Elution Buffer and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute to elute the DNA.
Prepare the samples by mixing 5 parts of DNA Sample with 1 part of gel loading dye.
Load 2 µL of each sample per well and include 5 µL of the 1 kbp ladder in each run.
Run the gel at 80 V for 01:00:00 .
Reagent setup: Tris Buffer: Dissolve 89 mg of Tris-OH and 42 mg of Tris-HCl in 100 mL H 2 O.
Pipette 40 µL Mastermix into every PCR tube/well, then add 2 µL of template.
Vortex the AMPure XP beads for 00:00:30 to ensure an even dispersion. Then, transfer the appropriate volume of bead solution into a reagent reservoir based on the number of samples being processed.
Using a multichannel pipette, add 20 µL of AMPure XP beads to each well of the amplicon PCR plate.
Seal the plate and shake at 1800 rpm, Room temperature, 2 minutes in the Thermomixer.
Incubate at Room temperature without shaking for 00:05:00 .
Place the plate on a magnetic stand for 00:02:00 or until the supernatant has cleared.

With the amplicon PCR plate on the magnetic stand perform an ethanol wash: Use a multichannel pipette to remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to remove any beads. Wash the beads with freshly prepared 80% ethanol: Using a multichannel pipette, add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each sample well. Incubate the plate on the magnetic stand for 00:00:30 . Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.

Perform a second ethanol wash: Using a multichannel pipette, add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each sample well. Incubate the plate on the magnetic stand for 00:00:30 . Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
Remove the amplicon PCR plate from the magnetic stand. Using a multichannel pipette, add 52.5 µL of 10 millimolar (mM) Tris pH 8.5 to each well of the amplicon PCR plate.
Seal the plate and shake at 1800 rpm, Room temperature, 2 minutes , ensuring the beads are fully resuspended.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .
Place the plate on the magnetic stand for 00:02:00 or until the supernatant has cleared.
Using a multichannel pipette, carefully transfer 50 µL of the supernatant from the amplicon PCR plate to a new 96‐well PCR plate. Ensure no beads are transferred.
Measure the concentration using Nanodrop1000 and dilute the samples to 20 ng/µL as needed.
Send the tubes to an external sequencing service and await the data.
creator: Philip Laric
